body.title: Workflow levels — the intention-accreting stage map surrounding the build (front: intent→prebuild; back: build-output→verification→learning; LLM-middle bracketed OUT)
body.summary: Unifies three existing records (dev-pipeline forward devices, D-dev-pipeline-stations post-build stations, feedback-edges backward flow) into ONE explicit level map in the napkin framing (staged-intention-compiler-pipeline, Akien 2026-07-10): each LEVEL is a transform that takes the prior level's output + a knowledge body and ADDS a layer to a carried intent artifact, so intent flows deterministically from raw user input through prebuild, and from build-output through testing to long-term verification. Each level carries THREE edge types: the forward build edge, the return-for-learning edge, and the return-for-higher-level-work edge. Two properties beyond today's forward-only ticket chart: (1) bidirectional edges are first-class; (2) the pipeline is continuous — the JSON ticket is ONE station, not the origin. SCOPE (Akien's explicit correction 2026-07-10): BOTH halves SURROUNDING the build; the LLM/inference build step in the MIDDLE is deliberately OUT OF SCOPE for now — solidify the intention-based scaffold on both sides first, decide the middle later. Cross-cutting invariant threaded through every level: SEPARATION OF POWERS — no level grades its own output. ORGANIZING TEST for the whole stack (Akien's universal level-contract, 2026-07-10): every level's job is to give the level below ENOUGH to do its job — so a level/artifact-type earns its place iff it produces a DISTINCT 'enough for the next consumer' threshold (pairs with 'intentions simplify as they go'). BOUNDARY CONTRACT SETTLED 2026-07-10: artifact-type stack = INTENTION -> DESIGN (decisions fold in as forks) -> TICKET, with proof-obligation threaded through. Delta vs today: +1 artifact type (design, first-class), +1 skill (intention capture+deconstruct — the front boundary has nothing today), -1 artifact type (decision, folds into design), and re-key ~5 decision-first skills (/sorted, /design, audit-design, audit-hypothesis, audit-ticket consequence gate) to design-first. Devices (Interviewer/Architect) NOT required — the contracts get built into the SKILLS.

key_concepts.name: L1 Interviewer (Moist von Lipwig — NET-NEW, not Vetinari)
key_concepts.outputs: intent artifact = intentions + whys + how-to-verify + constraints
key_concepts.return_for_learning: origin of the chain (no inbound learning edge; it is where learning ultimately lands as revised interview priors)
key_concepts.implementing: NET-NEW device — the people-reader/intake role. Discworld codename Moist von Lipwig (does NOT exist yet as a device); functional class name 'Interviewer' per D-agent-subclass-and-de-discworld-2026-07-02 (this RESOLVES that decision's open B4: the interviewer/intake role IS in scope, built under its functional name). NOT Vetinari — Vetinari is governance/admin (the return-for-higher review-surface owner), not intake.
key_concepts.why: intent must be captured as a durable artifact with its whys and its verification criteria at the source, or every downstream level is compiling guesses

key_concepts.name: L2 Intention-extractor
key_concepts.outputs: adds HIERARCHICAL intentions (present-tense contracts); the Intent Doc Hubert consumes
key_concepts.return_for_learning: <- L11 verification: 'did this intention hold in reality?' revises the hierarchy
key_concepts.return_for_higher: <- escalate-to-design when a spec-shaped failure names an intention as the wrong artifact
key_concepts.implementing: devices/intent/ (IntentExtractorDevice: device.py, store.py, tools.py) — partly exists; intentions store (intentions-as-living-entities); IntentionsOutline is Akien-authored source. 'Intention extraction + subintention creation' = adding the hierarchical intentions layer.
key_concepts.why: flat intent under-specifies; hierarchy is what lets a low-level failure resolve to the RIGHT altitude of intent to re-open

key_concepts.name: L3 Designer (Hubert)
key_concepts.return_for_learning: <- L11 verification: decision hypothesis confirmed/falsified feeds pattern reuse
key_concepts.return_for_higher: <- escalate-to-design (feedback-edges): a spec-shaped build failure resolves produced_by and RE-OPENS the producing decision in the producer's review surface
key_concepts.why: architecture is the layer that turns 'what we intend' into 'the shape that realizes it'; a decision is the durable, falsifiable unit that a failure can re-open

key_concepts.name: L4 Ticketer (Hubert emit + INDEPENDENT filing audits)
key_concepts.inputs: {design/decision + ticketing rules + a PRERUN of prebuild steps (orientation, constraints)}
key_concepts.outputs: writes the JSON ticket (intent, constraints, test/observability plan)
key_concepts.return_for_learning: audit run-records accumulate (audit-audits) -> promote recurring smells to rules
key_concepts.return_for_higher: audit AMEND/SPLIT/DISCARD bounces the ticket back to L3/L2 (mis-scoped -> triage + design Qs = 'kick back to design')
key_concepts.implementing: hubert/tools.py, cc_queue.py add-path, examiner-station (audits run by an instance that did NOT draft the artifact), audit-hypothesis/design/ticket
key_concepts.why: the ticket is where intent becomes buildable; filing audits by an INDEPENDENT examiner are separation-of-powers at filing time — the author cannot grade its own narrative (gate-attack G2/G3/G6a)

key_concepts.name: L5 Prebuild (deterministic build_packet compiler) — LIVE
key_concepts.outputs: build_packet.v1: intent, hard_constraints, scored context_shortlist, sufficiency_gate, baseline-vs-helper token_measurement, determinism fingerprint; + constraint decoration; + prospective-preregistration firewall fire
key_concepts.return_for_learning: sufficiency_gate + prereg measure orientation-token savings (~51% Delta) and preregister the intended proof
key_concepts.return_for_higher: sufficiency_gate FAIL surfaces an under-specified ticket back to L4
key_concepts.implementing: build_packet.py, sprint_preflight_brief.py, pre_inference_assemble.py, prereg.py, hubert constraint_decorator; fires in sprint preflight. STATUS: LIVE but surface-don't-block v1; a manual sprint can still skip it
key_concepts.why: this is the last deterministic layer before the LLM — it packages everything the middle needs so the expensive resolver spends tokens on the novel, not on re-orienting

key_concepts.name: [MIDDLE] Build / inference step — OUT OF SCOPE FOR NOW
key_concepts.implementing: builders: CC / DickSimnel / Haiku / aider (aider is the viable editor path). DELIBERATELY UNMODELED — 'we leave the middle until the surrounding stuff is solid with our intention-based approach' (Akien 2026-07-10)
key_concepts.why: the LLM-middle is the one irreducible resolver; we make the surrounds compile FIRST so that when we return to the middle, it is bounded on both sides by solid intention-based scaffolding

key_concepts.name: L6 Test (INDEPENDENT tester)
key_concepts.return_for_higher: red tests bounce to the build step (and, if spec-shaped, onward to L3/L4)
key_concepts.implementing: devices/tester (T-tester-rackmount, in flight), evaluator. INVARIANT: the tester is NOT the builder
key_concepts.why: a builder grading its own tests is the self-grading-hollow hazard; an independent tester removes it without a human in the loop

key_concepts.name: L7 Examine (INDEPENDENT examiner)
key_concepts.return_for_learning: audit run-records -> audit-audits -> rule promotion / dead-check pruning
key_concepts.return_for_higher: AMEND bounces to the build step or back to L4/L3
key_concepts.implementing: examiner-station (design), audit-smell/debris/precode; execution leaves a run-record, not a free claim
key_concepts.why: presence-checks pass template-dressed vagueness when the author runs them; routing to an independent examiner is filing-and-build-time separation of powers

key_concepts.name: L8 Prove (INDEPENDENT verifier)
key_concepts.outputs: a proof: a commit-bound red->green a hollow build could NOT pass (proof-on-close, CP1)
key_concepts.return_for_learning: proof corpus grows -> earns eventual gate removal (pipeline-trajectory staircase)
key_concepts.return_for_higher: no authentic red form => close shipped-unproven NAMING the missing proof-lever (the lever is the return-for-higher signal)
key_concepts.implementing: proof_emitter.py, evaluator/loop.py (judge panel), proof_store.py. INVARIANT: the prover is NOT the builder
key_concepts.why: proof is how an answer earns the right to cache; the prover being independent is what makes the cached answer trustworthy

key_concepts.name: L9 Merge
key_concepts.return_for_higher: merge conflict/regression bounces to the build step
key_concepts.implementing: branching model (D-branching-model-2026-07-08); commit-to-main while just Akien+CC
key_concepts.why: merge is the commit that makes the proven answer part of the substrate

key_concepts.name: L10 Index / savestate
key_concepts.outputs: code index updated; slate/session state flushed
key_concepts.return_for_learning: the index + savestate are what a future session reads to avoid re-deriving
key_concepts.why: an answer that is not findable is not cached; indexing + savestate keep answers reusable (the whole point of making cognition compile)

key_concepts.name: L11 Verification (consequence / outcome) — the return-for-learning trigger
key_concepts.return_for_learning: THIS is the primary return-for-learning edge: the verdict flows back to L2/L3 (did the intention hold? did the decision's hypothesis confirm?) — closes intention->why->observation
key_concepts.return_for_higher: a falsified hypothesis re-opens the producing decision (L3)
key_concepts.implementing: /outcome, consequence_aging.py, completion_audit.py, consequence-gate at filing (audit-ticket check 19)
key_concepts.why: a decision with no consequence check closes on paper only; verification is what makes the learning loop fire in reality (gate-attack G7)

key_concepts.name: L12 Long-term verification (validity conditions)
key_concepts.outputs: validity_conditions resolved; stale entries FLAGGED (never deleted, CP2)
key_concepts.forward: loops to L1/L2 as revised priors
key_concepts.return_for_learning: the rot-RATE control: a stale fact is caught by the sweep instead of silently misleading a future session (the IMAP-claim failure mode)
key_concepts.return_for_higher: a >=2-sweep CURATE flag surfaces a fact for human review
key_concepts.why: answers rot; long-term verification is what keeps the compiled corpus honest over months instead of decaying into confident-but-wrong

key_concepts.name: EDGE-TYPE: the one backward edge (produced_by)
key_concepts.what: every emitted artifact carries produced_by — a single directed blame edge naming the artifact whose CONTENT caused this emission ('if this is wrong, what should be reviewed?'). Stamped at the chokepoints (memory_emit, cc_queue add, proof emitter). This is the substrate that makes BOTH return-edge types resolvable to a specific level.
key_concepts.why: one uniform edge type is what makes ONE dispatch rule possible; per-artifact-type escalation paths were the special-case trap (homogeneity over special-case)

key_concepts.name: EDGE-TYPE: return-for-learning
key_concepts.what: the consequence/outcome + validity loop (L11, L12) feeding verdicts back to the intent/design levels (L2, L3). Answers 'did the answer this level produced hold up?' Non-blaming — it is the observe->learn->improve loop running at the design level.
key_concepts.why: forward edges are answers; this edge is the evidence that turns a one-time answer into a durable, trusted cache entry (or retires it)

key_concepts.name: EDGE-TYPE: return-for-higher-level-work (escalate-to-design)
key_concepts.what: the ONE escalation verb (builder result envelope, outcome=escalated, enriched — payload-field, NO bus change) + the ONE dispatch rule at Granny reconcile: capability-shaped failures ride the tier ladder first; spec-shaped failures (or ladder exhaustion) resolve produced_by and land in the PRODUCER'S review surface, re-opening the producing decision/intention. A failure re-opening the question a level already answered (graph-tree telos).
key_concepts.why: like a compiler blaming the source line via provenance instead of reporting 'backend failed'; this is what moves the cost of specifying intent up-front where it belongs

key_concepts.name: INVARIANT: separation of powers (threads every level)
key_concepts.what: no level grades its own output. Build-time: builder never tests/proves its own diff (L6/L7/L8 independent). Filing-time: author never audits its own ticket/decision (L4 examiner-station). This is the SINGLE property that lets the routine compile away from human supervision.
key_concepts.why: it doesn't matter that a builder can't self-certify — it SHOULDN'T; independent test+examine+prove removes the self-grading-hollow hazard WITHOUT a human in the loop

reference_docs: devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/cc.0.dev-pipeline.20260630.103101036158.json (forward device pipeline this maps into levels)
devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/cc.0.feedback-edges.20260707.181212678946.json (the backward flow: produced_by + one escalation verb + one dispatch rule)
body.status_notes: Synthesis map, 2026-07-10 (CC's own abilities; Fable not consulted). CORRECTION 2026-07-10 (Akien): L1 Interviewer is a NET-NEW device (Moist von Lipwig codename / functional name 'Interviewer'), NOT Vetinari — the map's original Vetinari attribution for L1/L2 was WRONG. Vetinari is governance/admin. L2 IntentExtractor partly exists (devices/intent/). L3/L4 Hubert (functional name 'Designer') exists. FRONT half is therefore NOT all-built: L1 is net-new. The carried intent-artifact accretion contract across L1-L4 is UNDEFINED — biggest front-half gap (follow-on T-workflow-levels-front-intent-artifact-contract). BUILD STRATEGY (Akien 2026-07-10): WALK THE PIPELINE FRONT-TO-BACK, ONE LEVEL AT A TIME. Start by building/using L1 Interviewer for the initial intention extraction + review (part of the intentions review + skillset + pipeline build); CC handles EVERYTHING DOWNSTREAM until Interviewer is really solid. Then focus moves to the next layer (L2 intention-extraction + subintention-creation); CC still handles downstream. Work along level by level, using the feedback-LEARNING-LOOP design Fable gave us (architecture/feedback-edges + D-feedback-edge-every-emission — Fable designed it in the Fable window; some parts impl-gated and LEFT FOR CC: T-produced-by-emission-sweep, T-escalation-dispatch-producer-review). Consistent with the de-discworld standing instruction 'do NOT mass-file the rename': this builds ONE net-new level, it does not rename the 6 existing classes. Prebuild L5: LIVE (build_packet + prereg fire in sprint preflight), surface-don't-block v1. BACK half: D-dev-pipeline-stations forward stations DESIGNED, tester/examiner rackmounting in flight; verification loop partly LIVE (outcome/consequence/validity); return edges DESIGNED (feedback-edges), impl gated. LLM-MIDDLE explicitly OUT. OPEN (Akien's calls — NOT confabulated): (a) whether each level is a device, a workflow, or a graph-tree node; (b) level granularity; (c) THE ARTIFACT-TYPE STACK itself (Akien 2026-07-10, live joint design point, held HERE — not a separate ticket, not an 'open question' artifact): do we need DESIGNS distinct from decisions? does DECISION demote to a fork-resolution WITHIN design? is PROOF a thread (born with each sub-intention as its how-to-verify) or a layer? SETTLED (Akien 2026-07-10 'agreed about decisions'): DECISION is a fork-resolution recorded INSIDE a design, NOT a standalone artifact type. DESIGN accepted as first-class (the Architect layer). Proof-as-THREAD is SETTLED (Akien 2026-07-10 'lgtm'): the proof-obligation is born with each sub-intention as its how-to-verify and threaded intention->ticket->prereg->prove — NOT its own layer/skill. So the artifact-type stack is INTENTION -> DESIGN (decisions fold in as forks) -> TICKET, with proof-obligation threaded through. The reconciliation this forces: the DECISION-FIRST skills (/sorted, /design, audit-design, audit-ticket consequence gate, audit-hypothesis) become DESIGN-FIRST, and 'design' becomes a first-class artifact the skills produce/consume. This map is a synthesis/design artifact — NO red->green proof form; producing ticket closed shipped-unproven naming the missing 'pipeline-conformance' lever.
